revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Fujishima Shrine]][[fr:Fujishima-jinja]][[ja:藤島神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Fukui, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fujishima Shrine
藤島神社 | image = File:Fujishima Shrine in Fukui city.jpg | caption = Fujishima-jinja | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.056419|N|136.210958|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} | founded_by = {{ill|Matsudaira Mochiaki|en|Matsudaira Mochiaki|qid=Q6788042|lt=Matsudaira Mochiaki|1=Matsudaira Mochiaki}} | established = 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 3-8-21 Moya, Fukui-shi, Fukui-ken 918-8003 Japan | website = {{URL|http://fujishima-jinja.jp}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Fukui|qid=Q201618|1=Fukui (city)}} | part_of = {{ill|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|qid=Q10289770|1=List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} }} {{nihongo|'''Fujishima Shrine''' |藤島神社|Fujishima-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|qid=Q201618|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}}, Japan. In the former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], it was a special shrine (別格官幣社, ''Bekkaku Kanpei-sha''). Its main festival is held annually on August 25. {{nihongo|'''Nitta Yoshisada'''|新田 義貞||1301 – August 17, 1338}} was the head of the {{ill|Nitta clan|en|Nitta clan|qid=Q2274150|lt=Nitta clan|1=Nitta clan}} in the early fourteenth century, and supported the [[Southern Court]] of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. He fought against the partisans of the [[Northern Court (Japan)|Northern pretender]] led by {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} brothers in a see-saw campaign which saw the capital change hands several times. However, during the {{ill|Sieges of Kuromaru|en|Sieges of Kuromaru|qid=Q7510591|lt=Battle of Kuromaru|1=Sieges of Kuromaru}} in 1338 he was killed in combat. In 1660, a farmer tilling the land near the site of the battle uncovered a ''{{ill|Kabuto|en|Kabuto|qid=Q1720878|lt=Kabuto|1=Kabuto}}'' helmet and presented it to {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsumichi|en|Matsudaira Mitsumichi|qid=Q11530368|lt=Matsudaira Mitsumichi|1=Matsudaira Mitsumichi}}, ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Fukui Domain|en|Fukui Domain|qid=Q903537|lt=Fukui Domain|1=Fukui Domain}}. The construction of the helmet indicated that it had belonged to a high-Ranking warrior, and the domain's chief military strategist, Inoue Banzaemon declared that it must have belonged to Nitta Yoshisada. A mound, the "Nitta-zuka", was built on the site where the farmer found the helmet as a memorial. In 1870, the imperial governor of Fukui, {{ill|Matsudaira Mochiaki|en|Matsudaira Mochiaki|qid=Q6788042|lt=Matsudaira Mochiaki|1=Matsudaira Mochiaki}}, built a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on top of the Nitta-zuka. This shrine was named "Fujishima Jinja" in 1876 and was given a formal Ranking in the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} system. However, the shrine was relocated in 1881, and rebuilt again in 1905, so that its present location is some three kilometers from the original Nitta-zuka. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]], built by the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|qid=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} to commemorate the events of the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} and to promote loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]]. The ''kabuto'' is preserved at the shrine, and is an Important Cultural Property.{{cite web |url= http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kougei/fujishimajinja-ginzogan.html |title= 福井県の文化財|trans-title= Fukui Prefecture Cultural Properties|language=Japanese |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=}} ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Official website|1=http://www.fujishima-jinja.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} [[File:Sujikabuto at Fujishima shrine.JPG|thumb|right|''Kabuto'' of Nitta Yoshisada]] [[en:Fujishima Shrine]] {{Commons category|Fujishima-jinja (Fukui)}} [[fr:Fujishima-jinja]] [[ja:藤島神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11623940}} [[Category:1870 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukui (city)]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
